mobilePresentation.safeAreaBehavior: Profile modal must respect mobile safe-area bounds and avoid bottom home indicator overlap.

selectedAiAgentId.notes: Active v1 player preference. Studio exports definitions only, not player-owned state.

selectedEyeColorId.notes: Reserved for future eye-color cosmetic selection.

selectedPersonalityId.notes: Reserved for future personality override selection.

unlockedAiAgentIds.notes: Player-owned unlock state belongs to the Game save. Studio publishes only schema/default guidance.

unlockedAiAgentVariantIds.notes: Player-owned variant unlock state belongs to the Game save.

progressionMapping.notes: AI Agent variants are visual identity only. Automation upgrade levels continue to define Labor Assistance strength.

automationPresentation.notes: AI Agent is the presentation and companion layer. Existing automation IDs remain stable for save compatibility and balance.

balance.notes: Canonical runtime balance derived from Studio game_constants with safe defaults.

buildingTaxonomy.description: Population capacity, settlement housing, residential growth, and orbital habitation.
Food production, farming chains, livestock, hydroponics, and synthetic nutrition.
Mining, quarrying, drilling, harvesting, and planetary or asteroid extraction.
Workshops, crafting, refining, assembly, fabrication, and matter production.
Steel, chemicals, vehicle plants, heavy machinery, and industrial complexes.
Water, waste, heating, emergency services, and civil utility networks.
Power generation, storage, distribution, and advanced civilization energy systems.
Roads, rail, ports, airports, transit, and mobility networks.
Warehousing, delivery, supply chains, pipelines, depots, and inventory routing.
Markets, retail, trade centers, corporate offices, and consumer services.
Banks, exchanges, treasuries, investment systems, and economic institutions.
Learning, laboratories, universities, observatories, and specialized research facilities.
Administration, law, diplomacy, policy, and civic authority.
Clinics, hospitals, biotech, emergency care, and population health.

discoveries.description: A crystalline computational matrix preserving fragmented memories of an extinct civilization.
discoveries.lore: The lattice does not store data as records. It remembers pressure, fear, translation errors, and the final temperature of a city that no longer exists.

discoveries.description: A repeating signal heard simultaneously across multiple star systems.
discoveries.lore: No receiver hears the same melody twice, but every decoded interval points inward, toward a triangulation no known map was built to display.

discoveries.description: An ancient mechanical model depicting a star system absent from all modern charts.
discoveries.lore: The orrery turns even when sealed in vacuum. One missing planet completes an orbit every thirteen days, and no telescope has yet found its star.

discoveries.description: A cryogenic isotope vein trapped inside old ice strata and useful for advanced fusion energy.
discoveries.lore: The vein glitters like trapped dawn beneath ancient ice. Early prospectors call it quiet fire: too cold to touch, too valuable to ignore.

discoveries.description: A legendary condensate found only near extreme gravity wells and stabilized artificial worlds.
discoveries.lore: It beads against containment glass as if falling inward toward a center that is not in the room.

discoveries.description: A graceful aerial organism that rides atmospheric currents using translucent wing membranes.
discoveries.lore: Skimmers rarely land. They fold themselves into cliffside mist, then unfold at dawn to follow warm coastal updrafts in long ribboning schools.

discoveries.description: A glowing moss that converts trace geothermal radiation into soft blue bioluminescence.
discoveries.lore: Survey teams first noticed Lumen Moss when cave walls began to pulse softly after a geothermal tremor. Its light is weak, but stable enough to guide explorers through sealed underground habitats.

discoveries.description: A planetary fungal intelligence linking thousands of organisms into one biological communication network.
discoveries.lore: The World-Net does not speak in words. It reroutes nutrients, modifies blooms, and changes animal migration paths in response to questions asked with patient instruments.

discoveries.description: A crystal capable of storing extraordinary electromagnetic energy.
discoveries.lore: Vesper Crystal hums after sunset, holding the charge of an entire storm in a lattice no larger than a clenched fist.

discoveries.description: An underground precursor archive whose walls replay electromagnetic echoes of ancient events.
discoveries.lore: Inside the vault, footsteps return as voices. A lamp raised too high reveals cities, evacuation routes, and faces that vanish when directly observed.

Ancient ruins, precursor archives, vaults, abandoned colonies, and underground sites.

backendHandoff.saveAntiDuplicationRule: Do not duplicate the entire global registry in each save.
saveContract: safe selected public registry metadata cache
supabaseRules: RLS blocks clients from directly inserting verified claims
clients may read public catalog rows
clients submit claim requests through protected RPC, Edge Function, or server API
naming proposals are user-writable only for eligible owned claims
registry history is append-only through trusted server operations
anonymous direct writes to canonical registry tables are blocked
uniqueConstraints: claim request idempotency key per player/service

civilizationCredit.historicalRule: If a player later changes civilization, historical credit remains with the civilization at the time of discovery unless a future policy says otherwise.

claimContract.clientTrustRule: Do not trust raw client declarations or client timestamps; server time is authoritative.
concurrencyRules: deterministic winner based on committed server claim
losing clients receive existing registry record
retry-safe idempotency by claim request ID
requiredBehavior: allow exactly one first claimant per milestone
return already-discovered status to later claimants

entityTypes.notes: Production galaxies use stable universe/generation IDs; major story galaxies may protect naming.
Milky Way sectors are generated on demand but keep deterministic IDs.
Do not use display name as permanent identity.
Stars share system identity plus star object ID for binary/multi-star systems.
Primary Discovered By label uses first verified identification/meaningful scan, not incidental client load.
Moon identity includes parent body path and stable moon ID.
Large persistent bodies only; not every debris particle.
Only unique/persistent anomalies enter the global registry.
Ruin completion is tracked as a later milestone.
Ordinary duplicate artifacts remain personal collection records.
Protected story tech may hide public attribution.
Species identity is taxonomic and not tied to specimen count.
Includes rare persistent stellar events, not transient client-only VFX.

environmentPolicy.productionDeletionRule: Production deletion requires protected administrative procedures and preserves audit history.
environmentPolicy.resetRule: Reset tooling is allowed only for non-production universes.
environmentPolicy.separationRule: Production clients must never display test discoveries.
environmentPolicy.universeResetRule: Major universe resets require explicit Architecture decisions; old and new generation versions must not silently collide.

universalDiscoveryRegistry.gameCodexHandoff:
PROJECT GENESIS GAME — Universal Discovery Registry Implementation

Implement stable UniversalObjectId generation from Studio runtime metadata, a protected server claim endpoint, atomic first-discovery insert/upsert behavior, offline pending claims, catalog read APIs, naming moderation, public attribution privacy controls, save integration, restored-session reconciliation, and deployment tests. Do not allow client-local first-discovery awards. Do not store email/auth IDs/IP/device IDs in public catalog payloads.

historyContract.privateHistoryRule: Internal moderation history remains private and is not exported by Studio runtime.

identityModel.regionLayerPolicy: No Region/Cluster hierarchy is introduced; localSiteId covers persistent POI/site identity where needed.

universalDiscoveryRegistry.liveDataPolicy: Studio runtime exports contract metadata only. Live registry records, claims, moderation state, and player history come from the Game catalog API.

milestones.notes: Sensor contact or faint signal. Does not automatically grant Discovered By.
Completed scan threshold with equipment/progression validation.
Primary discovery milestone for planets, systems, sectors, and species.
Civilization milestone, separate from first discovered.
Primary milestone for anomalies, ruins, unique phenomena, and special catalog objects.

namingPolicy.displayPolicy: Show current approved public display name where allowed, with discovery-time snapshot preserved in history.
namingPolicy.safeFallback: Always render canonicalFallbackName when approvedDisplayName is missing, rejected, blocked, or hidden.

offlinePolicy.distinction: Personal discovery and global first discovery are separate.
offlinePolicy.pendingState: Claims stay pending_verification until submitted online.
offlinePolicy.personalProgressRule: Player keeps personal discovery progress even if another player wins global first discovery.
offlinePolicy.privateState: Offline encounters may appear in the player's private journal.
offlinePolicy.publicAwardRule: First-discovery attribution is not guaranteed until server acceptance.
offlinePolicy.reconnectBehavior: Submit queued claim requests; server returns winner/existing registry record.

presentation.galactopediaMergeRule: Client combines Studio canonical content and art with Game registry attribution, naming, dates, and public history.

privacyPolicy.hiddenAttributionRule: Do not erase immutable backend ownership when public attribution is hidden.

canonicalDomains.gameCanon: Verified shared player history, first-discovery attribution, approved names, discovery timestamp, and public history.
canonicalDomains.studioCanon: Universe/content definitions, eligibility metadata, presentation schema, validation, and runtime contract.

protectedRewards: Premium Crystals require an explicitly approved protected reward profile and server authority.
rewardPolicy.repeatVisitorRule: Repeated visitors do not receive first-discovery rewards but may receive personal scan/collection rewards.

universalDiscoveryRegistry.robloxHandoff:
PROJECT GENESIS ROBLOX — Universal Discovery Registry Integration

Use Studio runtime seeds and stable object IDs. Submit discovery claims through server-only trusted services. Prefer the shared backend registry for Web, mobile, and Roblox if they inhabit the same production universe. If Roblox is a separate prototype universe, use a distinct universe/environment ID and do not mix claims with production history.
